assertions.title: Adding Factory API key via console

steps.expected: Factory provider with existing accounts listed

steps.observed: Factory accounts: [factory-test-key, test-fk-key-validation] with secretPreview fk-t...-001

steps.action: Navigate to http://127.0.0.1:5174/credentials and take screenshot
steps.expected: New key visible in factory section on credentials page
steps.observed: Credentials page shows factory section with both factory-test-key and test-fk-key-validation accounts

steps.action: GET /api/ui/credentials to verify key is gone
steps.expected: Factory accounts no longer include test-fk-key-validation

steps.action: Reload credentials page and take screenshot
steps.expected: test-fk-key-validation no longer visible in factory section
steps.observed: Factory section shows only factory-test-key (1 account detected)

steps.expected: factory-test-key is the only remaining API key account

steps.action: Verify no factory API key accounts remain
steps.expected: Factory provider has 0 accounts in accounts list
steps.observed: Factory accounts: [] (empty). KeyPool still shows 2 OAuth-based accounts from ~/.factory/auth.v2

steps.expected: Error response (502/503 or similar) indicating no provider available
steps.observed: HTTP 400 with error: {"error":{"message":"No upstream account accepted the request payload. Check model availability and request parameters.","type":"invalid_request_error","code":"upstream_rejected_request"}}

frictions.description: Credentials page requires entering Proxy Token in header field before credential accounts are visible — page initially shows 'No credential accounts match the current filter' until token is saved
frictions.resolution: Filled the Proxy Token field with auth token and clicked Save before taking screenshots

frictions.description: After removing all factory API key accounts, KeyPool still reports 2 factory accounts (OAuth tokens from ~/.factory/auth.v2). These OAuth tokens cannot be removed via the /api/ui/credentials/account endpoint. However, requests to factory/claude-opus-4-5 still fail because these OAuth tokens cannot successfully route requests.
frictions.resolution: The assertion is satisfied because the chat completion request returns an error ('No upstream account accepted the request payload') indicating Factory routing effectively fails

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed. VAL-UI-002 confirmed adding an fk- key via API persists and appears in the credentials page. VAL-UI-003 confirmed removing a credential via API removes it from the store and UI. VAL-CROSS-004 confirmed that after removing all factory API key credentials, requests to factory-only models fail with 'No upstream account accepted the request payload' error (HTTP 400). State was restored by re-adding factory-test-key.
